records.ety: [L. co- + incidere to fall on; in + cadere to fall: cf. F. concider. See Chance, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coincided (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Coinciding.]

defs: To occupy the same place in space, as two equal triangles, when placed one on the other.
quotes: If the equator and the ecliptic had coincided, it would have rendered the annual revoluton of the earth useless.

defs: To occur at the same time; to be contemporaneous; as, the fall of Granada coincided with the discovery of America.
To correspond exactly; to agree; to concur; as, our aims coincide.
quotes: The rules of right jugdment and of good ratiocination often coincide with each other.
